items.summary: Rewrite the Gemini API prompt in frontend/src/services/analysisApi.ts to be a minimal, direct instruction focused solely on predicting the next candle direction. The new prompt must: (1) tell Gemini to look at the image and find the candlestick chart area, ignoring everything else on the screen; (2) examine the last 5 visible candles and the overall trend; (3) predict whether the NEXT candle is more likely to be bullish or bearish; (4) return ONLY a valid JSON object with these fields: sinal ('COMPRA' if next candle likely bullish, 'VENDA' if likely bearish, 'SEM ENTRADA' if unclear), tendencia ('ALTA'/'BAIXA'/'LATERAL'), confianca (number 0-100), padroes (array of short Portuguese pattern name strings, max 3 items), explicacao (one sentence in Portuguese explaining the prediction). The prompt must be short (under 200 words), use plain language, and explicitly state that Gemini must respond with raw JSON only — no markdown, no code blocks, no extra text.

Update the Gemini API response parsing logic in frontend/src/services/analysisApi.ts to robustly extract JSON from the Gemini response text. Gemini sometimes wraps JSON in markdown code fences (```json ... ```) or adds surrounding text. The parser must: strip any markdown code fence wrappers before JSON.parse; attempt to extract the first {...} block from the response text if direct parsing fails; log the raw response text to the console for debugging; throw a descriptive AnalysisApiError with Portuguese message 'Resposta inválida da IA — tente novamente' if no valid JSON can be extracted.

Update the image-to-base64 conversion step in frontend/src/services/analysisApi.ts to ensure the image is always sent as a clean base64 string. Remove the base64 data URI prefix (e.g., 'data:image/jpeg;base64,') before placing the value in the inlineData.data field — Gemini Vision API requires raw base64 data without the data URI prefix. Also ensure the mimeType field is correctly set to the actual file MIME type (e.g., 'image/jpeg', 'image/png', 'image/webp') and never hardcoded to a fixed value.
